feed.title: John Misczak
feed.subtitle: A blog about hardware, software, and everything in between.

entry.title: Tracking Temperature and Humidity at Home with Time Series Data

link.title: Tracking Temperature and Humidity at Home with Time Series Data

entry.summary: Disclaimer: I am a MongoDB employee. Background Several years back, I received a Raspberry Pi 3 Model B+ kit from CanaKit alongside this book. I spent some time doing the basic stuff with it (blinking LEDs, running a Linux server, etc.) but eventually turning it into a RetroPie and installing lots of retro games on it. After a while, I lost interest in tinkering with it and just let it collec...

entry.title: Building Event Driven Experiences with MongoDB Realm and AWS

link.title: Building Event Driven Experiences with MongoDB Realm and AWS

entry.summary: Disclaimer: I am a MongoDB employee. Most developers by now are familiar with MongoDB, a NoSQL database that stores data as documents instead of rows. MongoDB’s fully managed service product, MongoDB Atlas, comes with MongoDB Realm, which is a set of services that help facilitate mobile and web development by providing a scalable, serverless backend for your application. Realm offers a lot of ...
